title: dysoxylum alliaceum
intro: Dysoxylum alliaceum, also known as the Alligator Wood or Garlic Tree, is a species of tree native to Australia and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Dysoxylum alliaceum include treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from pain and swelling. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of extracts, oils, and infusions, which are often used in traditional remedies and modern herbal supplements.
